theme: routers with frozen tables outlive any cable cut
title: Frozen Tables, Severed Cables
seed_problem: static next-hop tables scored by worst single-edge-failure delivery
mutation: composite: couple static per-(node,destination) forwarding tables with a full single-edge-failure sweep and a loop-freedom-bounded delivery simulation; the objective becomes the worst-case count of stranded ordered pairs, minimized -- turning routing quality into a property of the induced per-destination forwarding DAG rather than of shortest-path lengths.
strategies: trivial: shortest-path next hop, no backup (= the checker's reference baseline) -> ratio ~0.1; any cut tree edge strands its whole subtree

greedy (obvious recipe): shortest-path primary plus a classic loop-free alternate = a STRICTLY-CLOSER backup neighbor; protects nodes with a second closer neighbor but leaves same-distance 'rung' layers unprotected

insight (strong): a backup neighbor is valid iff its own primary route to d avoids the protected node (arc-disjoint in the forwarding DAG), even if it is NOT strictly closer -- accept the longer detour to survive the cut

beyond strong: co-design primaries away from shortest paths so every node gains two arc-disjoint downhill escapes (redundant/independent trees), pushing worst-case stranded pairs toward the unavoidable bridge floor
